label: Transparent Scree Slope motif source
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: Morphous transparent motif cutout source
Primary request: Create only a Scree Slope motif for later background removal: an isolated cluster of angular mountain talus scree rocks, no surrounding landscape, no text.
Subject: a rugged pile of fractured grey stone shards and talus blocks with varied slate faces, chipped edges, rust-orange mineral stains, small pale green lichen patches, and a few cool sky-blue reflective highlights on sharp stone planes. The subject should read as mountain scree rocks, not a cliff wall.
Composition/framing: centered complete cutout with generous padding; do not crop the rock pile; make a strong readable silhouette suitable as a design-system motif asset.
Lighting/mood: clean studio light preserving grey stone planes, rust seams, lichen speckles, and cool alpine sky highlights; rugged navigation-system mood, precise and durable.
Transparent workflow: place the subject on a perfectly flat solid #ff00ff chroma-key background for removal. The background must be one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, or lighting variation. Do not use #ff00ff or magenta anywhere in the subject.
Constraints: no mountain vista, no snowfield, no plants except tiny lichen patches on rock, no path signs, no props, no labels, no watermark, no decorative background, no cast shadow, no contact shadow, no reflection.

label: Light-mode design-system board
prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous light-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate a comprehensive light-mode design-system board for a Morphous theme named "Morphous Scree Slope", derived from the visible scree rocks motif: angular grey mountain talus stones with rust mineral stains, lichen speckles, and cool alpine sky highlights.
Reference use: Extract palette, texture rhythm, contrast, spacing mood, and interaction language from fractured grey stone planes, rust-orange seams, lichen yellow-green flecks, pale sky blue highlights, charcoal cracks, and alpine mist neutrals. Use only these motif-derived colors plus near-neutrals from the same source.
Canvas: exact wide 16:9 board, 3840 x 2160 preferred, at least 2400px wide. Create a single polished source board, not separate pages.
Resolution and legibility: high-resolution 4K-class source board with large readable labels and clear UI examples; avoid dense tiny token tables.
Required visible sections: Morphous Scree Slope title, Palette, Type, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Dashboard/Data, Forms, Tables, Command/Search, Status, Tokens, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadows.
Typography: include English and Japanese examples; guidance for Geist or Inter paired with Noto Sans JP; rugged navigation-system tone, precise and utilitarian.
UI coverage: shadcn/tweakcn-compatible buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, table rows, command palette search, sidebar navigation, top nav, dashboard metric cards, bar/line/donut charts, empty/loading/error/success/warning states, form validation, compact data table, texture swatches, radius 8px, spacing scale, border tokens, focus rings, shadow tokens.
Visual style: premium rugged product UI system, precise grid, 8px card radius, crisp shadcn-style controls, slanted scree-edge dividers, contour-map navigation cues, stone-chip texture used sparingly, durable route-finding interface.
Palette labels to show: Summit Mist, Slate Plane, Rust Traverse, Lichen Pin, Sky Break, Charcoal Crack, Talus Shadow, Stone Face, Alpine White, Route Amber.
Text: short readable UI labels only; use real section labels listed above; avoid long paragraphs.
Constraints: no colors outside the motif palette, no generic purple or blue gradients, no mountain photo background, no random brand names, no watermark, no misspelling of "Morphous Scree Slope".

label: Dark-mode design-system board
prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous dark-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate a dark-mode companion board for the same Morphous theme named "Morphous Scree Slope". It must feel like the visible light board translated to dark mode, not a new theme.
Reference use: Use the visible scree rocks motif and the visible light-mode design-system board as references. Preserve fractured grey stone surfaces, rust traverse accents, lichen pins, sky break highlights, contour navigation cues, component coverage, spacing, and 8px radius.
Canvas: exact wide 16:9 board, 3840 x 2160 preferred, at least 2400px wide. Create one polished dark-mode source board.
Resolution and legibility: high-resolution board with large readable labels and clear shadcn/tweakcn UI examples; avoid dense tiny text.
Required visible sections: Morphous Scree Slope, Dark Mode, Palette, Type, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Dashboard/Data, Forms, Tables, Command/Search, Status, Tokens, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadows.
Typography: English and Japanese examples; Geist or Inter plus Noto Sans JP; compact product UI, rugged navigation-system tone.
UI coverage: buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, table rows, command palette search, sidebar navigation, top nav, dashboard metric cards, charts, empty/loading/error/success/warning states, form validation, compact data table, texture swatches, radius 8px, spacing scale, border tokens, focus rings, shadow tokens.
Visual details: deep charcoal-crack background, alpine-white foreground, slate-plane cards, rust traverse primary actions, lichen status chips, sky-break focus rings, talus-shadow borders, subtle stone-chip and contour-line texture, crisp durable controls for route planning.
Palette labels to show: Summit Mist, Slate Plane, Rust Traverse, Lichen Pin, Sky Break, Charcoal Crack, Talus Shadow, Stone Face, Alpine White, Route Amber.
Text: short readable UI labels only; use real section labels listed above.
Constraints: no neon colors, no pure black flat background, no unrelated saturated colors, no generic purple or blue gradients, no mountain photo background, no random brand names, no watermark, no misspelling of "Morphous Scree Slope".
